result.response_content_preview:
ACTION:
FinalizeIdea

ARGUMENTS:
{"idea": {"Name": "geometric_interference_cancellation_icl", "Title": "Geometric Interference Cancellation: Training-Free Subspace Orthogonalization for Robust In-Context Learning", "Short Hypothesis": "In-context learning (ICL) robustness degrades under distributional shifts because task-relevant query representations geometrically overlap with context-specific noise subspaces in the residual stream. We hypothesize that a training-free, cross-covariance-based orthogonalization step can isolate the invariant task manifold by projecting query activations onto the orthogonal complement of dominant context-noise directions. This direction is necessary because conventional variance-based filtering mistakenly discards low-variance but geometrically distinct task features. ICL is the ideal setting as it provides clean, controllable context-query pairs for direct subspace isolation without fine-tuning. Simpler interventions like prompt engineering or heuristic
